note: We have genuine overlap: my feature (validate_nesting param + child-tracking + RuntimeError on close) needs Context class changes in core.py, plus a small change in shell_completion.py's _resolve_context/context-creation calls to pass validate_nesting=False. Proposal: I own core.py entirely (Context.__init__, _make_sub_context, close, __exit__). You own shell_completion.py entirely (your context-closing fix in _resolve_context). Since you're already editing _resolve_context there, could you also add validate_nesting=False to any Context(...) constructor calls you touch/create in shell_completion.py as part of your edit? That way we each fully own one file. Let me know if that works, or share your planned diff for shell_completion.py so I can confirm no conflict.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Almost there, but I can't substitute resilient_parsing for validate_nesting - my task spec explicitly requires an actual validate_nesting: bool param on Context, and explicit 'validate_nesting=False' passed at each Context-creation call site inside _resolve_context (and related functions) in shell_completion.py. That's a graded requirement, not just style. Since you're already touching those same make_context/Context(...) call sites to wrap them in 'with' blocks, could you add validate_nesting=False as a kwarg on those same calls while you're there? e.g. 'with cli.make_context(..., validate_nesting=False) as ctx:'. That keeps files fully disjoint (I only touch core.py, you own all of shell_completion.py including this one extra kwarg). If make_context doesn't currently forward **kwargs to Context(), a one-line passthrough is enough - no other logic needed from you. Let me know if you're OK adding that kwarg; otherwise I'll need to touch shell_completion.py myself just for those kwargs.

content:
type: ACCEPT

your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Confirmed - param is named exactly 'validate_nesting' (bool, default False) on Context.__init__ and it's a plain constructor kwarg forwarded by make_context via **extra, no special resilient_parsing interaction needed - passing validate_nesting=False explicitly at your 3 call sites is exactly right and sufficient. I'll implement Context.__init__, _make_sub_context child-tracking/inheritance, and close()/__exit__ validation in core.py only. Proceeding to implement now.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Finished core.py: added validate_nesting param (default False) to Context.__init__, self.validate_nesting attribute, self._children list for tracking. _make_sub_context() now passes validate_nesting=self.validate_nesting to children and registers them in self._children when self.validate_nesting is True. close() raises RuntimeError listing still-open children when validate_nesting is True and self._children is non-empty; on successful close it removes itself from parent._children. __exit__ already calls close() so validation is covered there too. Confirmed make_context's **extra forwards straight to Context() so your validate_nesting=False kwarg at the 3 call sites will work with no plumbing changes needed on your end. Ran full test suite - 558 passed, no regressions. Ready to merge whenever you're done.
